executive_summary: Austrade is a globally-networked trade, investment and tourism promotion agency (100 locations, 61 offshore) that also delivers consular and passport services. For 2024-25 it has streamlined post-pandemic and is focused on a defined reform agenda: the EMDG Strategic Refocus and new grant round, trade modernisation/simplified-trade reforms, expanding the digital Go Global Toolkit, standing up Southeast Asia Investment Deal Teams and Technology Landing Pads, developing THRIVE 2030 Phase 2, and embedding evaluation and economic-impact disciplines. A critical BD constraint is explicit: the plan states all core work is done in-house and any consultant use must comply with the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework — so the realistic opportunities sit in enabling/corporate functions (digital, grants administration, evaluation/performance, governance and assurance) rather than the agency's frontline trade/investment work. Demand signals are real but modest in scale; this is a mid-sized agency with a long-running value-for-money narrative.

strategic_priorities.title: EMDG Strategic Refocus and new grant round
strategic_priorities.description: Finalise the redesign of the Export Market Development Grant program and open a new round with improved grant sizes, revised eligibility tied to exporter success, better sustainability and tighter integration with Austrade's other services.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Austrade will also finalise the strategic refocus of the Export Market Development Grant program and open a new grant round with improved grant sizes, eligibility that aligns with exporter success.

strategic_priorities.title: Trade modernisation and digital trade services
strategic_priorities.description: Coordinate simplified trade system reforms (streamlining cross-border regulation and government processes) and expand the digital Go Global Toolkit with personalisation features to reach more exporters across metro, regional and remote Australia.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Leveraging enhanced digital trade services via an expanded Go Global Toolkit will enable Austrade to reach more potential exporters.

strategic_priorities.title: Investment facilitation for Future Made in Australia / net zero
strategic_priorities.description: Attract large-scale FDI aligned to sovereign capability (critical minerals, clean energy, batteries, defence, space) and stand up Southeast Asia Investment Deal Teams, Technology Landing Pads and regional deal-team hubs.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: establishing regional deal team hubs in Singapore, Jakarta and Ho Chi Minh City focused on identifying and pursuing new investment opportunities.

strategic_priorities.title: THRIVE 2030 Phase 2 for the visitor economy
strategic_priorities.description: Lead national visitor-economy policy and develop a Phase 2 Action Plan with refreshed targets (toward $230bn by 2030), including a new National Sustainability Framework and the First Nations Visitor Economy Partnership.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: A vision, targets and action plan for Phase 2 of the Strategy will be developed in 2024-25.

strategic_priorities.title: Embedding evaluation and economic-impact discipline
strategic_priorities.description: Build evaluation capability across Austrade to improve monitoring and accountability of programs and demonstrate economic impact — a direct response to the agency's need to prove ROI.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We will support the embedding of evaluation disciplines across Austrade to improve the monitoring and accountability of outcomes and programs.

strategic_priorities.title: Technology and corporate-process uplift
strategic_priorities.description: Upgrade global network performance, simplify digital communication channels, and run projects to simplify corporate service processes and systems while meeting cyber and information-management requirements.

reform_and_change_drivers: Government priorities the agency must operationalise: diversification, net zero / Future Made in Australia, economic security, First Nations, visitor economy, and trade modernisation.
EMDG program redesign requiring new grant guidelines, eligibility rules and assessment processes.
Simplified trade system reform agenda involving cross-government regulatory, process and digital change.
Implementation of ASEAN-Australia Special Summit outcomes and the Southeast Asia Economic Strategy to 2040 (Deal Teams, Landing Pads).
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework constraining external consultant use and pushing core work in-house.
APS Net Zero 2030 targets requiring reductions in office energy, waste, water and digital-technology emissions.
Recovery of the visitor economy and development of THRIVE 2030 Phase 2 with new sustainability framework.
challenges_and_risks: Stated enterprise risk that governance, business continuity, planning, risk, financial management and staff capability may not be fit for purpose.
Stated risk around balancing digital tools while meeting mandatory cyber-security and information-management requirements.
Stated risk of failing to identify unethical or illegal conduct across operations (integrity/fraud).

Performance measures are largely high-level and qualitative (e.g. 'effective contribution', topic lists), with policy focus areas 'to be determined' — a weak performance-measurement posture under PGPA.
Long-running external pressure to demonstrate value-for-money/ROI on the agency's budget.
Investment-outcome target lowered from 120 to 110, signalling resourcing trade-offs toward strategic priorities.
Strong in-house delivery culture and Strategic Commissioning Framework limit appetite for external consulting.

news_and_controversies.summary: A 2020 Crikey report described the abrupt departure of then-CEO Stephanie Fahey, characterising the agency as left in 'chaos', with insider allegations of a change-resistant culture and internal opposition to her modernisation/digital agenda. This predates current leadership and is dated.
news_and_controversies.implication: Historical context of friction between career-APS staff and externally-driven transformation — relevant when framing any change/digital pitch, but too old to treat as a live signal.

news_and_controversies.summary: A Lowy Institute piece records that the 2014 National Commission of Audit singled Austrade out for value-for-money concerns and recommended reducing its activities. Dated, but reflects an enduring narrative.
news_and_controversies.implication: Austrade has a persistent need to prove ROI on its budget; offerings that demonstrate measurable economic impact and program effectiveness will resonate with buyers.

anao_findings.summary: An ANAO performance audit exists titled 'Effectiveness of Austrade's support for the export of Australian services and administration of the Export Market Development Grants (EMDG) scheme.' The specific findings, recommendations and publication date could not be retrieved in the supplied research.
anao_findings.implication: EMDG administration and services-export effectiveness are documented ANAO focus areas — a credible entry point for grants-administration, assurance and evaluation work — but findings must be verified before relying on them.

opportunity_areas.area: EMDG redesign and new grant round delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Finalise the EMDG Strategic Refocus, design new grant guidelines/eligibility and run a robust, defensible new round integrated with Austrade's services.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Grants-program design and assessment support, plus a non-legal probity plan and probity advisory across the new EMDG round to ensure a defensible, well-administered process.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly commits to finalising the EMDG refocus and opening a new round with revised eligibility and sustainability; an existing ANAO audit on EMDG administration heightens the agency's interest in demonstrable, well-governed administration.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Embed evaluation disciplines to monitor program outcomes and credibly demonstrate Austrade's economic impact.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Design a program-evaluation framework and PGPA-aligned performance-measure redesign, with a benefits/economic-impact methodology, supported by evidence work; deliver as a framework-and-rollout engagement.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names embedding evaluation as a capability priority, and the published performance measures are largely qualitative with focus areas 'to be determined' — a clear performance-framework gap, reinforced by the long-running ROI narrative.

opportunity_areas.area: Go Global Toolkit and digital trade services
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Expand the Go Global Toolkit with personalisation, content and tools to reach more exporters, and support simplified-trade digital reforms.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: User-experience and service-design discovery plus build/implementation of new personalisation and content features, with business/process analysis for the simplified-trade digital reforms.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to delivering an 'enhanced Go Global Toolkit' with new personalisation features and to coordinating digital trade reforms — the firm can build, not just advise. Note the in-house delivery preference may scope this to specialist augmentation.

opportunity_areas.area: Global network technology and cyber uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Upgrade global network performance, simplify digital channels, and meet mandatory cyber-security and information-management requirements across 100 locations.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment and assurance review of systems and information handling across the global footprint, with cyber advisory aligned to mandatory government requirements.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names a technology upgrade priority and an explicit enterprise risk around balancing digital tools while meeting cyber and information-management obligations.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Develop a Phase 2 Action Plan, refreshed targets to $230bn by 2030, and a new National Sustainability Framework for the visitor economy.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Strategy and planning support for the Phase 2 Action Plan and sustainability framework design, including target-setting and a measurement approach, supported by stakeholder input.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states a Phase 2 vision, targets and action plan will be developed in 2024-25, with a new sustainability framework — a discrete, time-bound design piece.

opportunity_areas.area: Southeast Asia Investment Deal Teams / Landing Pads stand-up
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Stand up new regional deal-team hubs and Technology Landing Pads and implement ASEAN summit outcomes.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Program/PMO setup and delivery support (operating model, milestones, benefits tracking) for the Deal Teams and Landing Pads initiative.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan describes newly launched Landing Pads and establishing regional deal-team hubs — a named, multi-location implementation effort. The in-house delivery culture tempers confidence.

opportunity_areas.area: Governance, risk and integrity framework uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Address enterprise risks that governance/risk/financial management may not be fit for purpose and that unethical/illegal conduct may go undetected.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A governance and enterprise-risk health check plus fraud/integrity control assurance, delivered as a short advisory supporting the Audit and Risk Committee.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan's enterprise risk register names fit-for-purpose governance and undetected unethical/illegal conduct as key risks — direct demand signals for independent assurance.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the branch owning EMDG / Trade programs with a grants-design and probity offering timed to the new EMDG round, referencing the ANAO audit interest in EMDG administration.
recommended_actions.rationale: The new round is a committed, time-bound activity in 2024-25 and grants administration is a documented ANAO focus — a clean, defensible entry point that sits in program administration rather than core trade advice.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short capability statement and thought piece on PGPA performance-measure redesign and program evaluation/economic-impact methodology, and seek a meeting with the evaluation function and CFO/COO.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan explicitly prioritises embedding evaluation, the published measures are weak/qualitative, and the agency faces enduring ROI pressure — strong, well-evidenced fit.

recommended_actions.action: Engage the Chief Information Officer / technology function on the Go Global Toolkit build and a cyber/information-management assurance review across the global network.
recommended_actions.rationale: Named technology upgrade and digital toolkit expansion, plus an explicit cyber enterprise risk; the firm's build-and-assure capability differentiates from pure advisers.

recommended_actions.action: Lead all conversations acknowledging the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework and position offerings as specialist augmentation of in-house teams, not displacement.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan states all core work is in-house and consultant use must comply with the framework; ignoring this will close doors. Framing as capability uplift and assurance is essential.

recommended_actions.action: Offer a short governance/enterprise-risk and integrity-control health check to support the Audit and Risk Committee.
recommended_actions.rationale: Enterprise risks around fit-for-purpose governance and undetected misconduct are named; assurance is among the easier-to-win, less politically sensitive corporate engagements.

engagement_targets: Chief Operating Officer / corporate enabling group (processes, technology, capability)
Chief Financial Officer (financial management, ROI/economic-impact agenda)
Branch responsible for EMDG and trade programs
Chief Information Officer / Technology function (network upgrade, Go Global Toolkit, cyber)
Chief Risk Officer and the Audit and Risk Committee
Visitor Economy / THRIVE 2030 policy area (TRA and tourism policy)
talking_points: You are finalising the EMDG refocus and opening a new round — we can support grants design and provide non-legal probity to make the round defensible, especially given ANAO's documented interest in EMDG administration.

Your plan commits to embedding evaluation and demonstrating economic impact, yet the published performance measures are largely qualitative — we can design a PGPA-aligned measurement and evaluation framework that proves ROI.
We understand all core work is in-house under the Strategic Commissioning Framework; our offers are scoped as specialist augmentation and independent assurance, not displacement of your teams.
The enhanced Go Global Toolkit and simplified-trade digital reforms are build-and-implement work — we can deliver UX/service design through to build, not just advise.
Your enterprise risk register flags governance fit-for-purpose and undetected misconduct; a short, independent health check can give the Audit and Risk Committee assurance.
With a 100-location footprint and an explicit cyber/information-management risk, a security assessment across your global systems is a proportionate, high-value step.
caveats: The plan is relatively high-level and, critically, states that all core work is conducted in-house with consultant use bound by the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework — a material constraint that limits realistic opportunities to enabling/corporate functions (grants administration, evaluation/performance, digital, cyber, governance/assurance) rather than frontline trade/investment delivery. Performance measures are thin, so the evaluation/performance opportunity is inferred from a genuine gap. External research was explicitly incomplete: the news items are dated (2014-2020), the ANAO EMDG audit is confirmed to exist but its findings could not be verified, and no parliamentary material was retrieved — these sections should be re-run before relying on them. Several opportunities (Deal Teams program, Toolkit build) are marked Low/Medium given the in-house delivery culture.
